sn12.20:1.2: “Monks, I will teach you dependent co-arising & dependently co-arisen phenomena.
sn12.20:1.3: Listen & pay close attention. I will speak.”
sn12.20:1.4: “As you say, lord,” the monks replied.
sn12.20:2.2: From birth as a requisite condition comes aging-&-death.
sn12.20:2.3: Whether or not there is the arising of Tathāgatas, this property stands—this regularity of the Dhamma, this orderliness of the Dhamma, this this/that conditionality.
sn12.20:2.4: The Tathāgata directly awakens to that, breaks through to that.
sn12.20:2.5: Directly awakening & breaking through to that, he declares it, teaches it, describes it, sets it forth. He reveals it, explains it, makes it plain,
sn12.20:2.7: From birth as a requisite condition comes aging-&-death.
sn12.20:3.1: “From becoming as a requisite condition comes birth.…
sn12.20:3.2: “From clinging/sustenance as a requisite condition comes becoming.…
sn12.20:3.3: “From craving as a requisite condition comes clinging/sustenance.…
sn12.20:3.4: “From feeling as a requisite condition comes craving.…
sn12.20:3.5: “From contact as a requisite condition comes feeling.…
sn12.20:3.6: “From the six sense media as a requisite condition comes contact.…
sn12.20:3.7: “From name-&-form as a requisite condition come the six sense media.…
sn12.20:3.8: “From consciousness as a requisite condition comes name-&-form.…
sn12.20:3.9: “From fabrications as a requisite condition comes consciousness.…
sn12.20:3.10: “From ignorance as a requisite condition come fabrications.
sn12.20:3.11: Whether or not there is the arising of Tathāgatas, this property stands—this regularity of the Dhamma, this orderliness of the Dhamma, this this/that conditionality.
sn12.20:3.12: The Tathāgata directly awakens to that, breaks through to that.
sn12.20:3.13: Directly awakening & breaking through to that, he declares it, teaches it, describes it, sets it forth. He reveals it, explains it, makes it plain,
sn12.20:3.15: From ignorance as a requisite condition come fabrications.
sn12.20:3.16: What’s there in this way is a reality, not an unreality, not other than what it seems, conditioned by this/that.
sn12.20:4.2: Aging-&-death are dependently co-arisen phenomena: inconstant, fabricated, dependently co-arisen, subject to ending, subject to passing away, subject to fading, subject to cessation.
sn12.20:4.9: “The six sense media are dependently co-arisen phenomena.…
sn12.20:4.13: “Ignorance is a dependently co-arisen phenomenon: inconstant, fabricated, dependently co-arisen, subject to ending, subject to passing away, subject to fading, subject to cessation.
sn12.20:4.14: These are called dependently co-arisen phenomena.
sn12.20:5.1: “When a disciple of the noble ones has seen well with right discernment this dependent co-arising & these dependently co-arisen phenomena as they have come to be, it is not possible that he would run after the past, thinking,
sn12.20:5.2: ‘Was I in the past? Was I not in the past? What was I in the past? How was I in the past? Having been what, what was I in the past?’
sn12.20:5.3: or that he would run after the future, thinking,
sn12.20:5.4: ‘Shall I be in the future? Shall I not be in the future? What shall I be in the future? How shall I be in the future? Having been what, what shall I be in the future?’
sn12.20:5.5: or that he would be inwardly perplexed about the immediate present, thinking,
sn12.20:5.6: ‘Am I? Am I not? What am I? How am I? Where has this being come from? Where is it bound?’
sn12.20:5.7: Such a thing is not possible.
sn12.20:5.9: Because the disciple of the noble ones has seen well with right discernment this dependent co-arising & these dependently co-arisen phenomena as they have come to be.”
